description: Agent Bridge Protocol (ABP) + reusable connector/SDK. Protocol-first. See DESIGN.md and SPEC/abp-v1.md.
rules: One feature per session. Commit, update progress.txt, exit.
Never set status:passed without test output recorded in the session.
Never edit/remove existing feature entries; only append or flip status.
On a broken state, git reset to last green commit; do not patch on decay.
Reference implementation language: TypeScript (Node), schema validation via a JSON Schema validator.

features.title: ABP v1 normative spec
features.evidence: SPEC/abp-v1.md written; covers envelope, control+data planes, closed schemas, security model, conformance.
steps: Cover lifecycle, security invariants, errors, conformance, extensibility

features.title: Machine-readable closed schemas (envelope/event/action/error)
features.evidence: SPEC/schemas/{envelope,event,action,error}.json written; all parse as valid JSON (init.sh check). Superseded/relocated by F0.3 (layered: schemas/core/ + schemas/profiles/).
steps: Author JSON Schema 2020-12 for the security-critical closed types

features.title: Layered architecture revision (Core + World Profile) per design review
features.evidence: Adopted plan B (layering) after foundational review. SPEC/abp-v1.md rewritten: §0.3 Core/Profile split; §5 World Profiles (profile meta-schema, x-abp-trust trust annotation, open-object bounds, content-hash pinning no fetch-by-URL); resume event-seq cursor (§4.3.2); in-band roles_query/roles_list + claim-based bind authorization + host-pubkey pinning (§4.2); relative deadlines + idempotency (§4.3.3); message size/depth limits (§2); error codes profile_unsupported/profile_mismatch. Schemas relocated to schemas/core/{envelope,event,action,error,profile}.json + schemas/profiles/social/1.json. init.sh recurses. Verification: all 6 schemas parse (init.sh); social profile passes structural check against core/profile.json meta-schema; untrusted + client_authored annotations present (python structural check). External refs §0.2/§6/§9 preserved.
steps: Rewrite SPEC/abp-v1.md: layered arch, profiles, trust annotation, resume cursor, role discovery+claim, relative deadlines, idempotency, size/depth bounds, host pinning.
Relocate schemas to core/ + profiles/social/1.json; add core/profile.json meta-schema; add x-abp-trust + context bounds.
init.sh recurse; verify parse + meta-structural check.

features.title: Schema validator + remaining control-plane schemas + conformance tests
features.evidence: Authored all 10 remaining control-plane schemas in SPEC/schemas/core/ (hello, hello_ack [inlined profile + hash], roles_query, roles_list, pair_challenge/request/result, resume [last_event_seq], ping [shared with pong], bye). Built packages/validator (TypeScript, Node 24 type-stripping, ajv 2020-12): CoreValidator validates the closed envelope, dispatches payload by type, enforces §2 size(65536B)/depth(16) limits; data-plane validates Core event/action envelope only (profile composition deferred to F1.2); x-abp-trust registered as a no-op annotation keyword. Conformance suite: 25/25 pass (node --test) + tsc typecheck clean. Verified via ./init.sh: 16 schemas parse, typecheck clean, 25 pass / 0 fail. Covered: valid messages pass; unknown type/kind-field rejected; additionalProperties:false enforced; data-plane+resume require session; event requires seq; wrong abp version rejected; oversize/non-JSON/over-depth/non-object rejected. Fixed envelope.json comment->$comment and added resume to session-required set.
steps: Author remaining control-plane schemas (hello, hello_ack, roles_query, roles_list, pair_*, resume, ping, bye) per §4 field tables (hello_ack carries the inlined profile + hash; resume carries last_event_seq).
Validator lib that checks envelope + payload by type/kind (Core layer only here; profile composition is F1.2).
Conformance tests: unknown type/kind/fields rejected; valid messages pass; data-plane requires session; size/depth limits (§2) enforced.

features.title: World Profile loader + pinning + composed data-plane validation
features.evidence: packages/validator/src/profile.ts: ProfileLoader validates a profile doc against core/profile.json meta-schema and compiles every events[kind].data / actions[kind].data; canonicalize()+profileHash() give a stable sha256-base64url content hash (§5.5); pin() verifies recomputed hash == advertised (else profile_mismatch), checks document self-identifies as advertised id/version, then accepts only bundled/trusted profiles silently or via an approve() hook (else profile_unsupported); bundled set = SPEC/schemas/profiles/social/1.json. PinnedProfile.validateEvent/validateAction compose data-plane validation; AbpValidator = CoreValidator + PinnedProfile. Trust paths (x-abp-trust untrusted/client_authored) enumerated from the pinned profile into profile.trust.{events,actions}[kind] for F4.1/F4.2. Verified via ./init.sh: 39/39 tests pass (25 conformance + 14 profile) + tsc clean. Profile tests cover: social/1 pins silently w/ expected kinds; tampered doc -> profile_mismatch; wrong hash -> profile_mismatch; unknown profile -> profile_unsupported w/o approval, pins w/ approval; invalid doc -> bad_message; composed valid event/action pass; unknown kind + unknown data field + oversize content + world.context>maxProperties + Core-layer (missing seq) all rejected; untrusted/client_authored path enumeration exact.
steps: Load + validate a profile document against core/profile.json meta-schema.
Pin by content hash; reject mismatch (profile_mismatch); approval gate for unknown (id,version,hash).
Compose validation: event/action data validated against pinned profile events[kind].data / actions[kind].data.
Enumerate x-abp-trust:untrusted / client_authored field paths from the pinned profile (feeds F4.1/F4.2).
Unit: bundled abp.social/1 pins silently; tampered doc -> profile_mismatch; unknown kind/field rejected.

features.title: Transport (outbound wss baseline) + envelope (de)serialize
features.evidence: packages/client/src/transport.ts: WssTransport (outbound WebSocket via ws; EventEmitter). One ABP message per UTF-8 JSON frame. connect() resolves on open; send() validates then serializes; inbound #onFrame validates every frame before delivery (size<=65536 -> JSON parse -> validateMessage) and emits 'message'(msg,type) or 'invalid'(reason,errors). TLS posture enforced at construction: wss:// always ok; ws:// only for loopback host; other schemes rejected (§2). Pluggable Validate fn (default CoreValidator; AbpValidator can be swapped in post-pairing). Verified via ./init.sh (exit 0): 10/10 e2e tests on a loopback ws server + tsc clean. Covered: valid hello round-trips and is delivered parsed; unknown-type / oversize / non-JSON / binary inbound frames -> 'invalid' not 'message'; send() refuses invalid + pre-open send throws; ws://non-loopback and non-ws scheme refused; wss://remote accepted. Test runner uses --test-force-exit (loopback servers leave the loop non-empty).
steps: wss client; one message per frame
